channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: When bullying happens at work
item.description: Most of us assume bullying is something we age out of by middle school, high school at the latest. By the time you’re a professional—especially one wi...

item.title: This Single ChatGPT Prompt Can Do Hours of Market Research in Minutes—Here’s How
item.description: AI is a powerful way to do market research. A big update from ChatGPT makes things even easier.

item.title: New U.S. legislation could upend credit card loyalty programs. The Points Guy founder calls the reckoning un-American
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: The ‘Menopause Penalty.’ When biology meets broken work systems
item.description: After 50, too many women reduce their working hours, become trapped in lower-quality jobs, or exit the labor market altogether. Part-time employment b...

item.title: 3 Tips for Long-Term Success in an Instant Gratification World
item.description: A transactional mindset that focuses on immediate success rather than patience is leaking into every aspect of our lives.

item.title: A Waymo vehicle hit a child. What can we learn from the incident?
item.description: On January 23rd, outside an elementary school in Santa Monica, California, a Waymo vehicle hit a child . That’s what we know for sure. It sounds shock...

item.title: AI Ads Are Here. How The Knot Is Experimenting With ChatGPT’s New Offering
item.description: Chief marketing officer Jenny Lewis shares her strategy for advertising in the age of AI.

item.title: Why Mark Cuban’s Definition of Capitalism Should Be Embraced by Everyone (But Not for the Reason You Might Think)
item.description: Because to Cuban, capitalism means living the life you want to live.

item.title: AI could spark a new age of learning, but only if governments, tech firms and educators work together
item.description: Former European Commission president José Manuel Barroso and Efekta Education CEO Stephen Hodges on how AI can help resource-strained education system...

item.title: Elon Musk and Mark Cuban Disagree Over Whether Money Can Buy Happiness. Here’s What Science Says
item.description: It’s hard to feel bad for Elon Musk, but his miserable tweets are a reminder of the complicated truth about money and happiness.

item.title: New tax rules are in effect this season—and many filers don’t know about them
item.description: Tax filing season is in full swing, and while preparing your taxes can often be filled with stress, misplaced documents, and worries about proper fili...

item.title: Congress just passed a major housing bill. Will it actually lower your home price?
item.description: Housing affordability is a top concern for many Americans, and both chambers of Congress have been advancing legislation to help prospective homeowner...

item.title: The ultrawealthy have 3 big secrets on reducing taxes including the way they die
item.description: “Assets like stocks can quietly grow for decades and, when they’re inherited, the tax bill often disappears.”

item.title: Top Hollywood screenwriter warns TikTok’s new tool is at the gates: ‘I hate to say it. It’s likely over for us’
item.description: Seedance 2.0, which is only available in China for now, lets users generate high-quality AI videos using simple text prompts.

item.title: Amazon’s Ring decides maybe partnering with a police surveillance firm is a bad idea after wide revulsion at Super Bowl ad
item.description: The Super Bowl ad depicts a lost dog being tracked by cameras throughout a neighborhood using artificial intelligence.
